title: LinkedIn Jobs Scraper
description: Scrape LinkedIn job listings at scale — no API key, no login, no browser required. Supports batch search across multiple keywords and locations, plus full job details: description, seniority, employment type, job function, industry, applicant count, and salary when shown.

keywords.sectionDescription: Define your job search. Use single fields for a simple search, or use the list fields below for batch search across multiple keywords and locations in one run.
keywords.title: 🔍 Keywords
keywords.description: Job search keywords (e.g. 'python developer', 'data engineer', 'sales manager'). For a single search. Use Keywords List below for batch mode.

location.title: 📍 Location
location.description: Location to search (e.g. 'United States', 'London, UK', 'Remote'). For a single search. Use Locations List below for batch mode.

keywordsList.title: 📋 Keywords List (Batch Mode)
keywordsList.description: Run multiple keyword searches in one go. Each keyword is combined with every location in Locations List — e.g. ['python developer', 'data engineer'] x ['New York', 'Remote'] = 4 searches. Overrides the single Keywords field when provided.

locationsList.title: 🗺️ Locations List (Batch Mode)
locationsList.description: List of locations to search across in batch mode. Combined with every entry in Keywords List. Overrides the single Location field when provided.

geoId.title: 🌐 Geo ID (optional)
geoId.description: LinkedIn geographic ID for precise location targeting (e.g. 103644278 for United States). Overrides text location when provided.

companyFilter.title: 🏢 Company Filter
companyFilter.description: Only return jobs from specific companies. Enter company names or LinkedIn URL slugs (e.g. 'google', 'stripe', 'openai'). Leave empty to return jobs from all companies.

datePosted.sectionDescription: Narrow your results by date, job type, experience, work arrangement, and salary.
datePosted.title: 📅 Date Posted
datePosted.description: Filter jobs by how recently they were posted.

workType.title: 🏠 Work Arrangement
workType.description: Filter by on-site, remote, or hybrid.

salary.title: 💰 Minimum Salary (USD)
salary.description: Filter by minimum annual salary. Only applies to jobs where LinkedIn shows salary data.

fetchJobDetails.sectionDescription: Control output depth, result limits, and enrichment options. More detail = more requests = higher cost and runtime.
fetchJobDetails.title: 📄 Fetch Full Job Details
fetchJobDetails.description: Loads each job's detail page to extract: full description (text + HTML), seniority level, employment type, job function, industry, applicant count, and salary when LinkedIn shows it. Adds one request per job — higher cost and runtime, so enable only when you need these fields.

fetchCompanyDetails.title: 🏢 Fetch Company Details (Employee Count)
fetchCompanyDetails.description: Also opens each company's LinkedIn page to extract the registered employee count (companyEmployeeCount). Requires Fetch Full Job Details. Results are cached per company within the run to minimize extra requests. Note: company website and description are not available to guests and are not returned.

maxResults.title: 📊 Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum total job listings to return. LinkedIn caps search results at 1,000 per query. Free users are limited to 25 per run.

maxResultsPerSearch.title: 🔢 Max Results Per Search (Batch Mode)
maxResultsPerSearch.description: In batch mode (Keywords List x Locations List), this limits results per combination. Example: 3 keywords x 2 locations x 50 per search = up to 300 total results.

proxyConfiguration.sectionDescription: LinkedIn blocks many datacenter IPs. Residential proxies are strongly recommended for reliable results.
proxyConfiguration.title: 🔒 Proxy Configuration
proxyConfiguration.description: Proxy settings. Residential proxies give the best success rate. Without proxies, LinkedIn may block requests.
